2605.21850.title: ACC: Compiling Agent Trajectories for Long-Context Training
2605.21850.paper_summary: Recent development of agents has renewed demand for long-context reasoning capacity of LLMs. However, training LLMs for this capacity requires costly long-document curation or heuristic context synthesis. We observe that agents produce massive trajectories when solving problems, invoking tools and receiving environment observations across many turns. The evidence needed to answer the original question is thus scattered throughout these turns, requiring integration of distant context segments. Nevertheless, standard agent SFT masks tool responses and only trains turn-level tool selection, creating a supervision blind spot where these scattered signals go unused. We propose Agent Context Compilation (ACC), which converts trajectories from search, software engineering, and database querying agents into long-context QA pairs that combine the original question with tool responses and environment observations gathered across multiple turns, training the model to answer directly without tool use. This makes the dependencies between the question and the evidence explicit, enabling direct supervision of long-context reasoning over distant segments without additional annotation. ACC is a simple but effective approach that can be combined with any existing long-context extension or training method, providing scalable supervised fine-tuning data. We validate ACC on long-range dependency modeling tasks through MRCR and GraphWalks, challenging benchmarks requiring cross-turn coreference resolution and graph traversal over extended contexts. Training Qwen3-30B-A3B with ACC achieves 68.3 on MRCR (+18.1) and 77.5 on GraphWalks (+7.6), results comparable to Qwen3-235B-A22B, while preserving general capabilities on GPQA, MMLU-Pro, AIME, and IFEval. Further mechanism analysis reveals that the ACC-trained model exhibits task-adaptive attention restructuring and expert specialization.
2605.21850.paper_summary_zh: 最近代理的發展重新激發了對大型語言模型（LLMs）長期推理能力的需求。<lb/>然而，為了培訓LLMs具備這種能力，需要昂貴的長文檔整理或啟發式上下文合成。<lb/>我們觀察到，代理在解決問題時會產生大量的軌跡，調用工具並在多次回合中接收環境觀察。<lb/>因此，回答原始問題所需的證據散布在這些回合中，需要整合遙遠的上下文片段。<lb/>然而，標準的代理SFT遮蔽了工具的回應，只訓練回合層級的工具選擇，造成了一個監督盲點，使這些分散的信號未被利用。<lb/>我們提出了代理上下文編譯（ACC），它將來自搜索、軟件工程和數據庫查詢代理的軌跡轉換為長上下文的問答對，將原始問題與多次回合中收集的工具回應和環境觀察結合起來，訓練模型直接回答而不使用工具。<lb/>這使得問題與證據之間的依賴關係變得明確，能夠在不需要額外標註的情況下，對遙遠片段的長期推理進行直接監督。<lb/>ACC是一種簡單但有效的方法，可以與任何現有的長上下文擴展或訓練方法結合，提供可擴展的有監督微調數據。<lb/>我們通過MRCR和GraphWalks對ACC在長距離依賴建模任務上進行驗證，這些挑戰性基準要求跨回合的核心參考解析和在擴展上下文中的圖遍歷。<lb/>使用ACC訓練Qwen3-30B-A3B在MRCR上達到68.3（+18.1），在GraphWalks上達到77.5（+7.6），這些結果可與Qwen3-235B-A22B相媲美，同時在GPQA、MMLU-Pro、AIME和IFEval上保持一般能力。<lb/>進一步的機制分析顯示，ACC訓練的模型展現了任務自適應的注意力重組和專家專業化。
2605.21850.author: Qisheng Su et.al.
2605.21850.authors: Qisheng Su, Zhen Fang, Shiting Huang, Yu Zeng, Yiming Zhao, Kou Shi, Ziao Zhang, Lin Chen, Zehui Chen, Lijun Wu, Feng Zhao
